title: Setup Python Environment and Dependencies
description: Create virtual environment and install all required packages for federated contrastive learning, adversarial training, and privacy attacks.

steps.step1: Create virtual environment: python -m venv venv && source venv/bin/activate
steps.step2: Install PyTorch 2.0+ with CUDA: pip install torch torchvision torchaudio --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu118
steps.step3: Install adversarial attack libraries: pip install torchattacks==3.4.0
steps.step4: Install ML utilities: pip install numpy scipy scikit-learn matplotlib seaborn pandas tqdm tensorboard
steps.step5: Install privacy/DP libraries: pip install opacus>=1.4.0
steps.step6: Verify GPU availability: python -c 'import torch; print(torch.cuda.is_available(), torch.cuda.get_device_name(0))'
steps.step7: Create output directories: mkdir -p results/{models,logs,figures,attacks} data/

title: Download and Prepare Datasets
description: Download CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100, create non-IID federated splits using Dirichlet distribution, and prepare data loaders.

steps.step1: Download CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 using torchvision.datasets, save to ./data/
steps.step3: Create contrastive learning augmentations: RandomResizedCrop(32, scale=(0.2, 1.0)), RandomHorizontalFlip(), ColorJitter(0.4,0.4,0.4,0.1), RandomGrayscale(p=0.2)
steps.step4: Generate non-IID splits using Dirichlet distribution with alpha=0.5 for 10 clients
steps.step5: Save splits as JSON: data/cifar10_dirichlet_alpha0.5_clients10.json
steps.step6: Create membership inference datasets: split each client's data into MEMBER (train) and NON-MEMBER (holdout) sets
steps.step7: Verify split statistics: plot class distribution per client to confirm non-IIDness

title: Implement Federated Contrastive Learning Framework
description: Build the core FCL infrastructure including FedAvg aggregation, SimCLR implementation, and client-server communication.

steps.step1: Implement ResNet-18 encoder with CIFAR-friendly modifications (kernel=3, stride=1 for first conv)
steps.step2: Implement SimCLR projection head: 2-layer MLP (512 -> 128) with ReLU, output dim=128
steps.step3: Implement InfoNCE loss with temperature tau=0.5, using cross-client negatives when available
steps.step4: Implement FedAvg aggregator: weighted averaging by client dataset size
steps.step5: Implement client training loop: local_epochs=5, batch_size=256, lr=0.001 with cosine annealing
steps.step6: Implement server orchestration: global rounds=50 for CIFAR-10, 30 for CIFAR-100
steps.step7: Save checkpoints after each global round to enable resumption

title: Baseline 1: Standard FCL (FedSimCLR)
description: Train standard federated contrastive learning without adversarial training or privacy defenses. Primary focus on CIFAR-10; CIFAR-100 for validation.

steps.step1: Train on CIFAR-10 with 10 clients, global_rounds=50, local_epochs=5, seeds=[42, 123, 456]
steps.step2: Train on CIFAR-100 with 10 clients, global_rounds=30, local_epochs=5, seed=[42] only (validation)
steps.step3: Save encoders as: results/models/baseline_fcl_cifar10_seed{seed}.pth
steps.step4: Log training curves: train_loss, contrastive_accuracy per round
steps.step5: Linear evaluation: freeze encoder, train linear classifier on full training set for 100 epochs, lr=0.1

title: Baseline 2: FCL + Adversarial Training (FCL-AT)
description: Train FCL with adversarial training using PGD. This is the strong baseline to demonstrate the privacy-robustness trade-off.

steps.step1: Implement PGD adversarial training for contrastive learning: perturb both views of a pair
steps.step2: Attack parameters: epsilon=8/255, step_size=2/255, num_steps=7 (PGD-7)
steps.step3: Train on CIFAR-10: global_rounds=50, local_epochs=5, seeds=[42, 123, 456]
steps.step4: Train on CIFAR-100: global_rounds=30, local_epochs=5, seed=[42] only (validation)
steps.step5: Save models: results/models/baseline_fcl_at_cifar10_seed{seed}.pth
steps.step6: Linear evaluation and standard accuracy reporting
steps.step7: Robustness evaluation: test accuracy under PGD-20 attack

estimated_time: 90 minutes (3 seeds CIFAR-10 + 1 seed CIFAR-100, ~2x slower)

title: Baseline 3: FCL + Differential Privacy (FCL-DP)
description: Train FCL with differential privacy using DP-SGD. Simplified to CIFAR-10 only with one epsilon value to save time.

steps.step1: Implement DP-SGD: max_grad_norm=1.0, noise_multiplier chosen for target epsilon
steps.step2: Target epsilon: epsilon=8 (moderate privacy level)
steps.step3: Calculate noise_multiplier using opacus privacy accounting for 50 rounds, delta=1e-5
steps.step4: Train FCL+DP on CIFAR-10 only: epsilon=8, seeds=[42, 123, 456]
steps.step5: Save models: results/models/baseline_fcl_dp_eps8_cifar10_seed{seed}.pth
steps.step6: Linear evaluation and standard accuracy reporting

title: Baseline 4: Naive Combination (FCL-DP-AT)
description: Naive combination of differential privacy and adversarial training. CIFAR-10 only, reduced to 1 seed for time.

steps.step1: Combine DP-SGD (epsilon=8) with PGD-7 adversarial training
steps.step2: Train only on CIFAR-10 with seed=[42] (single run for comparison)
steps.step3: Save models: results/models/baseline_fcl_dp_at_cifar10_seed42.pth
steps.step4: Linear evaluation: standard accuracy and robust accuracy under PGD-20
steps.step5: This baseline is expected to suffer significant utility loss (>10%)

title: FedSecure-CL: Full Framework
description: Train the proposed FedSecure-CL framework combining privacy regularization and adversarial training. CIFAR-10 is primary; CIFAR-100 for validation.

steps.step1: Implement Component 1: Adaptive contrastive loss with privacy regularization
steps.step2: Privacy regularization: L_privacy = -H(z) + H(z|membership) approximated via adversarial membership classifier
steps.step3: Implement membership classifier: MLP (128 -> 64 -> 2) predicting member/non-member
steps.step4: Full loss: L_total = L_contrastive + alpha*L_robustness + beta*L_privacy, alpha=1.0, beta=0.5
steps.step5: Component 2: Task-adaptive gradient noise (scale noise by gradient norm percentiles)
steps.step6: Train on CIFAR-10: global_rounds=50, local_epochs=5, seeds=[42, 123, 456]
steps.step7: Train on CIFAR-100: global_rounds=30, local_epochs=5, seed=[42] only

title: Ablation Study: Privacy Regularization Component
description: Evaluate the impact of the privacy regularization term by training without it (beta=0). CIFAR-10 only, 2 seeds.

steps.step1: Train FedSecure-CL without L_privacy (set beta=0), keeping alpha=1.0
steps.step2: This isolates the effect of adversarial training + gradient noise only
steps.step3: Train on CIFAR-10 only, seeds=[42, 123] (2 seeds to save time)
steps.step4: Save models: results/models/ablation_no_privacy_reg_cifar10_seed{seed}.pth
steps.step5: Compare privacy-robustness trade-off with full FedSecure-CL

title: Ablation Study: Gradient Perturbation Component
description: Evaluate the impact of task-adaptive gradient noise by training without it. CIFAR-10 only, 2 seeds.

steps.step1: Train FedSecure-CL without gradient noise (uniform noise or no noise)
steps.step2: Keep L_privacy and L_robustness active
steps.step3: Train on CIFAR-10 only, seeds=[42, 123]
steps.step4: Save models: results/models/ablation_no_grad_noise_cifar10_seed{seed}.pth
steps.step5: Measure impact on privacy leakage and utility

title: Ablation Study: Adversarial Training Component
description: Evaluate the impact of adversarial training by training FedSecure-CL without it (alpha=0). CIFAR-10 only, 2 seeds.

steps.step1: Train FedSecure-CL without adversarial training (alpha=0)
steps.step2: Keep L_privacy and gradient noise active
steps.step3: Train on CIFAR-10 only, seeds=[42, 123]
steps.step4: Save models: results/models/ablation_no_adv_train_cifar10_seed{seed}.pth
steps.step5: Measure privacy leakage (should be lower) but no adversarial robustness

title: Membership Inference Attack Evaluation
description: Evaluate privacy leakage using membership inference attacks (LiRA) on all trained models. Focus on CIFAR-10.

steps.step1: Implement LiRA (Likelihood Ratio Attack): shadow model-based attack
steps.step2: Train 3 shadow models per target (reduced from 5 to save time)
steps.step3: For each target model, compute membership scores using shadow model predictions
steps.step4: Metric: TPR at 0.1% FPR (standard for privacy evaluation)
steps.step5: Also report: AUC-ROC, attack accuracy at 50% threshold
steps.step6: Evaluate on CIFAR-10 models: all baselines and FedSecure-CL

title: Gradient Leakage Attack Evaluation
description: Evaluate vulnerability to gradient leakage attacks using Deep Leakage from Gradients (DLG). CIFAR-10 only.

steps.step1: Implement DLG attack: optimize dummy inputs to match gradients
steps.step2: Attack parameters: 500 iterations (reduced from 1000), lr=0.1, use L-BFGS
steps.step3: Select 30 random samples from test set for attack (reduced from 50)
steps.step4: Metric: PSNR between reconstructed and original images (higher = better privacy)
steps.step5: Also report: SSIM, L2 distance between reconstructions and originals

title: Adversarial Robustness Evaluation
description: Evaluate adversarial robustness using PGD and AutoAttack for models trained with adversarial training.

steps.step1: Implement PGD-20 attack: epsilon=8/255, step_size=1/255, num_steps=20
steps.step2: Test robust accuracy on CIFAR-10 test set (1000 samples for speed)
steps.step3: For best CIFAR-10 model, run AutoAttack (standard robustness benchmark)
steps.step4: Report: Standard accuracy, PGD-20 robust accuracy, AutoAttack robust accuracy
steps.step5: For CIFAR-100 models, use PGD-20 only

title: Statistical Significance Testing
description: Perform statistical tests to verify significance of findings.

steps.step1: Load all results from 3 random seeds for each method
steps.step2: Perform paired t-test between FCL-AT and FedSecure-CL for MIA TPR
steps.step3: Perform paired t-test for robust accuracy between FCL-AT and FedSecure-CL
steps.step4: Perform paired t-test for standard accuracy between baselines and FedSecure-CL
steps.step5: Report p-values, confirm p < 0.05 for statistical significance
steps.step6: Compute confidence intervals (95%) for all reported metrics

title: Privacy-Robustness Trade-off Visualization
description: Create figures showing the privacy-robustness-utility trade-off for all methods.

steps.step1: Plot 3D scatter: x=Standard Accuracy, y=Robust Accuracy, z=MIA TPR (lower is better for privacy)
steps.step2: Plot 2D trade-off curves: Robust Accuracy vs MIA TPR (Pareto frontier)
steps.step3: Plot 2D trade-off curves: Standard Accuracy vs MIA TPR
steps.step4: Color points by method, show error bars from multiple seeds
steps.step5: Highlight FedSecure-CL achieving better trade-off than baselines

title: Training Curves and Convergence
description: Visualize training dynamics and convergence behavior.

steps.step1: Plot contrastive loss curves over global rounds for all methods
steps.step2: Plot linear evaluation accuracy over training rounds
steps.step3: Plot learning curves showing convergence speed comparison
steps.step4: Create subplot grid comparing all methods
steps.step5: Save figures: results/figures/training_curves.png

title: Attack Results Visualization
description: Visualize membership inference and gradient leakage attack results.

steps.step1: Bar plot: MIA TPR at 0.1% FPR for all methods with error bars
steps.step2: ROC curves for membership inference (one per method, averaged over seeds)
steps.step3: Gradient leakage reconstruction quality: PSNR distribution across methods
steps.step4: Sample reconstructions from DLG attack for each method (visual comparison)
steps.step5: Save figures: results/figures/mia_results.png

title: Representation Analysis
description: Analyze learned representations using dimensionality reduction.

steps.step1: Extract features from test set using all trained encoders
steps.step2: Apply t-SNE (perplexity=30) to visualize feature space
steps.step3: Color points by class label and membership status
steps.step4: Compare: Standard FCL vs FCL-AT vs FedSecure-CL
steps.step5: Observe if FedSecure-CL reduces membership-related clustering

title: Success Criteria Verification
description: Verify all success criteria from the proposal are met.

steps.step1: Criterion 1: Verify FCL-AT has statistically significant higher MIA TPR than Standard FCL (p < 0.05)
steps.step2: Criterion 2: Compare FCL trade-off vs centralized baselines (from literature values)
steps.step3: Criterion 3: Verify FedSecure-CL outperforms baselines on CIFAR-10 (primary dataset)
steps.step4: Criterion 4: Verify FedSecure-CL achieves <5% utility loss vs Standard FCL
steps.step5: Criterion 5: Verify FedSecure-CL maintains >90% of FCL-AT robust accuracy
